filename: Dick, Philip K - Counter-Clock World (2012, Mariner Books_Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A novel set in a world where time has begun moving backwards: the dead rise from their graves, people grow younger, and food is un-eaten. Originally published in 1967, this Mariner Books/Houghton Mifflin Harcourt edition (EPUB format) preserves one of Dick's most conceptually playful explorations of entropy and temporal reversal.

filename: Dick, Philip K - Five Novels of the 1960s and 70s (2008, Library of America) - libgen.li.epub
display_title: Five Novels of the 1960s and 70s (Library of America)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: The second Library of America volume dedicated to Dick, collecting five novels from his most productive period: likely including Martian Time-Slip, Dr. Bloodmoney, Now Wait for Last Year, Flow My Tears the Policeman Said, and A Scanner Darkly. Dick's inclusion in the Library of America represents his canonical status in American literature. EPUB format.

filename: Dick, Philip K - Gather Yourselves Together (Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: One of Dick's early mainstream (non-SF) novels, written in the early 1950s but not published until 1994. Set in post-war China, the novel follows three Americans left behind at a factory compound as the Communist government takes over. Published posthumously by Houghton Mifflin Harcourt. EPUB format.

filename: Dick, Philip K - Now Wait For Last Year - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1966 novel about a drug called JJ-180 that sends users through time, set against an interstellar war between Earth and two alien races. Dr. Eric Sweetscent must navigate a dying marriage, a tyrannical world leader, and the destabilizing effects of temporal displacement. One of Dick's most admired middle-period novels. 131 pages in this PDF edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K - Our Friends from Frolix 8 (2013, Mariner Books_Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.azw3
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1970 novel set in a future where telepaths and precognitives form an oppressive ruling class. An ordinary man joins forces with a returning space explorer who brings an alien entity capable of destroying the elites' psychic powers. Mariner Books/Houghton Mifflin Harcourt edition in AZW3 format.

filename: Dick, Philip K - Solar Lottery (2012, Houghton Mifflin Harcourt_Mariner Books) - libgen.li.azw3
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: Dick's first published novel (1955), set in a future where the leader of the solar system is chosen by a random lottery. When a new Quizmaster is selected, the previous regime dispatches an assassin android. A fast-paced debut that introduces many of Dick's enduring themes. Houghton Mifflin Harcourt/Mariner Books edition in AZW3 format.

filename: Dick, Philip K - The Cosmic Puppets (Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: An early Dick novel (1957) in which a man returns to his hometown to find it completely transformed, with no one remembering the town he knew. Influenced by Zoroastrian mythology, the novel reveals a cosmic struggle between forces of light and darkness reshaping reality itself. Houghton Mifflin Harcourt EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K - The Game-Players of Titan (1963_2001, Vintage) - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1963 novel set on an Earth depopulated by alien war, where survivors play a Monopoly-like game called Bluff with real property stakes, overseen by telepathic alien Vugs from Titan. This Vintage 2001 reprint edition runs 212 pages. One of Dick's mid-career explorations of game theory, deception, and alien manipulation.

filename: Dick, Philip K - The Man in the High Castle (1962_2016, Vintage Books _ Boston _ Mariner Books _ Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.epub
display_title: The Man in the High Castle
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: Dick's Hugo Award-winning alternate history novel (1962) set in a world where the Axis powers won World War II. America is partitioned between Nazi Germany and Imperial Japan, and the I Ching guides both characters and author. Vintage/Mariner Books 2016 EPUB edition of Dick's most celebrated work.

filename: Dick, Philip K - The VALIS Trilogy (2011, Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.azw3
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: An omnibus edition collecting Dick's final three novels: VALIS (1981), The Divine Invasion (1981), and The Transmigration of Timothy Archer (1982). These works represent Dick's most autobiographical and theologically ambitious fiction, directly drawing on his 2-3-74 mystical experiences. Houghton Mifflin Harcourt edition in AZW3 format.

filename: Dick, Philip K - Ubik (2012, Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: Widely regarded as one of Dick's masterpieces, Ubik (1969) follows Joe Chip and a team of anti-psychics whose reality begins deteriorating after a catastrophic explosion. Objects regress to earlier forms, and a mysterious product called Ubik may be the only defense against entropic decay. Houghton Mifflin Harcourt 2012 EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - Clans of the Alphane Moon (1964, Ace Books) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1964 novel set on a moon colonized as a psychiatric hospital, where patients have organized into clans based on their diagnoses: Manses (manics), Heebs (hebephrenics), Pares (paranoids), Deps (depressives), Skitzes (schizophrenics), and Polys (polymorphous). Dick's satirical exploration of mental illness as social organization. Ace Books EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - Confessions Of A Crap Artist (1975, Ace Books) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: Dick's most successful mainstream (non-SF) novel, written in 1959 but not published until 1975. Set in Marin County, California, it follows Jack Isidore, a self-described crap artist who collects pseudo-scientific facts, and his sister Fay. A darkly comic domestic novel revealing Dick's literary range beyond science fiction. Ace Books EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - Dr. Bloodmoney, or How We Got Along After the Bomb (1977, Ace Books) - libgen.li.epub
display_title: Dr. Bloodmoney, or How We Got Along After the Bomb
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1965 post-apocalyptic novel set in the San Francisco Bay Area after a nuclear war, following a community of survivors dealing with mutants, a malevolent scientist (Dr. Bluthgeld), and an orbiting astronaut who serves as a DJ. One of Dick's richest ensemble novels. Ace Books 1977 EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - Mary And The Giant (1987, Ace Books) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A mainstream novel written in the 1950s and published posthumously in 1987. Set in a small California town, it follows a young woman's relationships with older men, including a Black jazz musician, against the backdrop of 1950s racial tensions and provincial conformity. Reflects Dick's ambitions as a realist literary novelist. Ace Books EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - The Cosmic Puppets (1957, Ace Books) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: The original 1957 Ace Books edition of Dick's early novel about a man who returns to his hometown to find it unrecognizably altered. Based on Zoroastrian cosmology, the novel reveals competing cosmic forces reshaping reality. This is a duplicate edition alongside the Houghton Mifflin Harcourt version. EPUB format.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - The Preserving Machine (1969, Ace Books) - libgen.li.epub
display_title: The Preserving Machine and Other Stories
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A short story collection first published in 1969, anchored by the title story about a machine that transforms musical compositions into living creatures to preserve them. The collection showcases Dick's inventive short fiction from the 1950s and 1960s. Ace Books EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - Ubik_ The Screenplay (2012, Houghton Mifflin Harcourt) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: Dick's own screenplay adaptation of his novel Ubik, written before his death in 1982 and published posthumously in 1985. Dick reimagined his novel for film, making significant changes to the narrative. A fascinating document of Dick's engagement with Hollywood and his vision for how his fiction should be adapted. Houghton Mifflin Harcourt 2012 EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip K. - Voices From The Street (2007, Ace Books) - libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A mainstream novel written in the early 1950s and published posthumously in 2007. Set in a California electronics store, it follows a young salesman's disillusionment and existential crisis. Draws heavily on Dick's own experience working at Art Music in Berkeley. One of the last Dick novels to reach print. Ace Books EPUB edition.

filename: Dick, Philip Kindred - A Maze of Death - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1970 novel in which colonists arrive on the mysterious planet Delmak-O, where they begin dying under strange circumstances. The novel layers multiple realities and features Dick's invented theology with entities called the Mentufacturer, the Intercessor, and the Walker-on-Earth. 104 pages in this PDF edition.

filename: Dick, Philip Kindred - The Game-Players of Titan - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: An alternate PDF edition of Dick's 1963 novel about a depopulated Earth where survivors play a high-stakes board game called Bluff, overseen by telepathic alien Vugs from Titan. 241 pages in this edition. Duplicate of the Vintage edition also in the archive.

filename: Dick, Philip Kindred - The Penultimate Truth - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1964 novel in which humanity lives in underground ant tanks, believing a nuclear war rages on the surface. In reality the war ended years ago, and the surface is a paradise controlled by an elite class using propaganda to keep the masses below. A prescient exploration of media manipulation and manufactured consent. 151 pages.

filename: Dick, Philip Kindred - The Simulacra - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1964 novel set in a future United States where the president is a simulacrum (android) and real power is held by a secret elite. Dick weaves together multiple storylines involving time travel, psychokinesis, and Martian emigration into a complex political satire about the artificiality of power. 222 pages.

filename: Dick, Philip Kindred - The Zap Gun - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1967 satirical novel about weapons designers on both sides of the Cold War who channel designs through mediumistic trances, producing weapons that are secretly converted into consumer products. When a real alien threat appears, the sham weapons are useless. A dark comedy about the military-industrial complex. 209 pages.

filename: Dick, Philip Kindred - Time Out of Joint - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1959 novel in which Ragle Gumm, a man who wins newspaper contests for a living, gradually discovers that his entire small-town 1950s existence is a constructed illusion. One of Dick's earliest explorations of simulated reality, anticipating The Truman Show by decades. 155 pages.

filename: Dick, Philip Kindred_ Zelazny, Roger - Deus Irae - libgen.li.pdf
author: Philip K. Dick and Roger Zelazny
card_summary: A collaborative post-apocalyptic novel by Dick and Roger Zelazny (1976). Set after nuclear devastation, a limbless painter undertakes a pilgrimage to paint the face of the Deus Irae (God of Wrath), the scientist who triggered the apocalypse. Blends Dick's theological concerns with Zelazny's mythological sensibility. 116 pages.

filename: Eye in the Sky{Philip K. Dick}(Houghton Mifflin Harcourt){114629893} libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A 1957 novel in which a group of people are knocked unconscious by a particle accelerator accident and awaken in successive solipsistic realities, each governed by one person's worldview: a religious fanatic, a paranoid, a prude, and a communist. An early Dick masterwork of subjective reality. Houghton Mifflin Harcourt EPUB edition.

filename: Nick and the Glimmung{Philip K Dick}(2009_2013, Subterranean){112816600} libgen.li.epub
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: Dick's only children's book, written in 1966 but not published until 1988. Nick and his family emigrate to the planet Plowman's Planet, where they encounter the Glimmung, a shape-shifting alien entity. Related to Dick's adult novel Galactic Pot-Healer. Subterranean Press 2009/2013 EPUB edition.
